title: Optimize Object Selection Speed on Migration/Dictionary Page
story: As a DataTools Pro user adding Salesforce objects to my Data Dictionary, I want the object selection process to be fast and responsive so that I do not experience frustrating load times when checking objects to add.
description:
The checkbox selection process for adding new Salesforce objects on the migration/dictionary page was slow and frustrating. The optimization defers field fetching until the user navigates to the object detail page, making the add-object step nearly instant.

On first-time detail view, fields are fetched from Salesforce with a loading message: "DataTools Pro is working, Your patience is greatly appreciated."
acceptance_criteria: Selecting an object checkbox to add it completes without noticeable delay
Salesforce field fetching is deferred until the user views the object detail page
First-time detail page view displays a loading message while fields are fetched
Subsequent detail page views load instantly from cached data
ux_changes: Object selection via checkbox is now instant. Detail page shows a loading message on first-time field fetch: "DataTools Pro is working, Your patience is greatly appreciated."
middleware_changes: Decoupled object selection from field fetching. Fields are now lazy-loaded only when the object detail page is accessed for the first time.
implementation_notes: The fix separates the object-add action from the field-fetch action. Adding an object no longer triggers a Salesforce API call for fields. Fields are fetched lazily on first detail page view.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented the lazy-loading approach and deployed to staging. The migration page object addition was confirmed to be much faster with fields deferred to the detail view.
